senses.definition: a written order directing a bank to pay money
examples: he paid all his bills by check

senses.definition: an appraisal of the state of affairs
examples: they made an assay of the contents
a check on its dependability under stress

senses.definition: the bill in a restaurant
examples: he asked the waiter for the check

senses.definition: the state of inactivity following an interruption
examples: the negotiations were in arrest
held them in check
during the halt he got some lunch
the momentary stay enabled him to escape the blow
he spent the entire stop in his seat

senses.definition: additional proof that something that was believed (some fact or hypothesis or theory) is correct
examples: fossils provided further confirmation of the evolutionary theory

senses.definition: the act of inspecting or verifying
examples: they made a check of their equipment
the pilot ran through the check-out procedure

senses.definition: a mark indicating that something has been noted or completed etc.
examples: as he called the roll he put a check mark by each student's name

senses.definition: something immaterial that interferes with or delays action or progress
a mark left after a small piece has been chopped or broken off of something
a textile pattern of squares or crossed lines (resembling a checkerboard)
examples: she wore a skirt with checks

senses.definition: the act of restraining power or action or limiting excess
examples: his common sense is a bridle to his quick temper

senses.definition: obstructing an opponent in ice hockey
(chess) a direct attack on an opponent's king
examine so as to determine accuracy, quality, or condition
examples: check the brakes
Check out the engine

senses.definition: make an examination or investigation
examples: check into the rumor
check the time of the class

senses.definition: be careful or certain to do something; make certain of something
examples: He verified that the valves were closed
See that the curtains are closed
control the quality of the product

senses.definition: lessen the intensity of; temper; hold in restraint; hold or keep within limits
examples: moderate your alcohol intake
hold your tongue
hold your temper
control your anger

senses.definition: stop for a moment, as if out of uncertainty or caution
examples: She checked for an instant and missed a step

senses.definition: put a check mark on or near or next to
examples: Please check each name on the list
tick off the items
mark off the units

senses.definition: slow the growth or development of
examples: The brain damage will retard the child's language development

senses.definition: be verified or confirmed; pass inspection
examples: These stories don't check!

senses.definition: be compatible, similar or consistent; coincide in their characteristics
examples: The two stories don't agree in many details
The handwriting checks with the signature on the form
The suspect's fingerprints don't match those on the gun

senses.definition: block or impede (a player from the opposing team) in ice hockey
develop (a child's or animal's) behavior by instruction and practice; especially to teach self-control
examples: Parents must discipline their children
Is this dog trained?

senses.definition: consign for shipment on a vehicle
examples: check your luggage before boarding

senses.definition: hand over something to somebody as for temporary safekeeping
examples: Check your coat at the door

senses.definition: abandon the intended prey, turn, and pursue an inferior prey
stop in a chase especially when scent is lost
examples: The dog checked

senses.definition: mark into squares or draw squares on; draw crossed lines on
decline to initiate betting
hold back, as of a danger or an enemy; check the expansion or influence of
examples: Arrest the downward trend
Check the growth of communism in South East Asia
Contain the rebel movement
Turn back the tide of communism

senses.definition: place into check
examples: He checked my kings

senses.definition: write out a check on a bank account
find out, learn, or determine with certainty, usually by making an inquiry or other effort
examples: I want to see whether she speaks French
See whether it works
find out if he speaks Russian
Check whether the train leaves on time

senses.definition: verify by consulting a source or authority
examples: check the spelling of this word
check your facts

senses.definition: arrest the motion (of something) abruptly
examples: He checked the flow of water by shutting off the main valve

senses.definition: make cracks or chinks in
examples: The heat checked the paint

senses.definition: become fractured; break or crack on the surface only
examples: The glass cracked when it was heated
